title: North Male Atoll Boat Rental: Best Charters for 2026
html:
North Male Atoll, with its turquoise lagoons and abundant coral reefs, is a premier destination for boat charters. Departing from Malé or the nearby Hulhumalé ferry terminal, expect to pay from around 6,500 MVR for a half-day shared tour. You can browse and book through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in North Male Atoll or list your own.

Where to set off in North Male Atoll

Most charters begin from the Villingili Ferry Terminal or the dedicated marina at Hulhumalé Lagoon. The North Male Atoll is dotted with anchorages such as Kuda Huraa, Kanifushi, and Baros Island. For a longer trip, many boats head towards the resort islands of Kuredu and Kuramathi. The calm inner lagoons are ideal for novice skippers, while the outer reefs offer more challenging waters for experienced sailors. You can rent a boat in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht, best for short island-hopping trips on a schedule, up to 8 guests from 12,000 MVR half-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran, stable and spacious for families, up to 12 guests from 15,000 MVR full-day.
  3. Speedboat, ideal for transfers between resorts and snorkelling spots, up to 6 guests from 4,500 MVR per trip.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht, all-inclusive with captain and crew, up to 10 guests from 35,000 MVR per day.

If you are considering a motor yacht, the Fairline 34 Targa model is a popular choice. You can review the specifications and availability for this motor yacht directly on the platform.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in North Male Atoll?

Prices start at 4,500 MVR for a shared tour and can exceed 40,000 MVR for a fully crewed luxury yacht. The exact cost depends on the boat size, duration, and whether you hire a skipper.

  • Shared tour (dhow or speedboat, 3-4 hours): from 4,500 MVR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4-6 guests): from 7,500 MVR.
  • Sailing catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from 15,000 MVR.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (up to 10 guests): from 20,000 MVR.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in North Male Atoll?

To rent an unskippered boat in the Maldives, you must hold a recognised international boating licence. Most local operators, including those listed on BoatHire24, will verify your credentials and may require a local permit for the atoll. If you prefer not to skipper, you can charter a crewed vessel with a licensed captain included.

The best time to charter in North Male Atoll is from November to April, when the northeast monsoon brings dry weather and calm seas. The local wind, known as Iruvai, creates ideal conditions for sailing and snorkelling.

At a glance

  • Price range: 4,500, 40,000 MVR per day
  • Peak months: November to April
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 guests on private charters
  • Currency: Maldivian Rufiyaa (MVR)

Questions about boat rental in North Male Atoll

Can I rent a boat for a day trip without a skipper?

Yes, if you hold a valid boating licence and the charter company agrees, you can hire a bareboat for a day.

Are there age restrictions for passengers?

Most charters welcome children, but life vests and safety briefings are mandatory for all ages.

What is included in the charter price?

Typically, the price includes fuel, basic insurance, and safety equipment; food and drinks may be extra unless specified otherwise.

Can I book a multi-day charter in North Male Atoll?

Yes, many operators offer overnight trips to remote islands and uninhabited sandbanks, often with accommodation on board.

Once you have chosen your vessel, you can finalise your booking and compare options directly on the platform. For the widest selection, browse the listings and filter by your preferred dates. Boat owners in the Maldives are welcome to list their boats on BoatHire24 to reach travellers from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in North Male Atoll and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
